workflow: Set review_decision to accepted or rejected.
Accepted entries must complete the curation block before fixture build.
Reviewed files can be passed to build_manual_fixture_from_review_candidates.py.

structured_payload_candidate.title: TAB – Minimum Fan Speed Determination
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Determine minimum speed setpoints for VFD-driven equipment (supply, return, relief fans) by starting at 6 Hz (10%) and increasing until visibly rotating. That speed becomes the minimum speed setpoint.
structured_payload_candidate.commissioning_check: Verify that minimum speed setpoints are determined per TAB specifications: start fan at 6 Hz (10%), observe rotation, increase if not rotating, record speed as minimum setpoint.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Minimum speed setpoints for all VFD-driven equipment shall be determined in accordance with the testing, adjusting, and balancing (TAB) specifications for the following, as applicable: 1. Supply fan 2. Return fan 3. Relief fan. There needs to be corresponding instructions in the TAB specifications. For example: 1. Start the fan or pump. 2. Manually set speed to 6 Hz (10%), unless otherwise indicated in control sequences. For equipment with gear boxes, use whatever minimum speed is recommended by the tower manufacturer. 3. Observe the fan/pump in the field to ensure it is visibly rotating. If it is not, gradually increase speed until it is. 4. The speed at this point shall be the minimum speed setpoint for this piece of equipment.

Manually set speed to 6 Hz (10%), unless otherwise indicated in control sequences. For equipment with gear boxes, use whatever minimum speed is recommended by the tower manufacturer. 3. The speed at this point shall be the minimum speed setpoint for this piece of equipment.

The speed at this point shall be the minimum speed setpoint for this piece of equipment.
candidate_entries.judge_reason: TAB procedure for minimum fan speed determination clearly detailed.
candidate_entries.review_notes: model_review_accepted: TAB procedure for minimum fan speed determination clearly detailed.

structured_payload_candidate.title: TAB – Minimum Outdoor Air DP Setpoint (Standard 62.1)
structured_payload_candidate.equipment_scope: Multiple-zone VAV AHU with separate minimum OA damper and DP control
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Establish DesMinDP by opening minimum OA damper fully, closing economizer damper, measuring OA flow, adjusting damper linkage if needed, and recording DP across OA damper at design minimum flow.
structured_payload_candidate.commissioning_check: Verify DesMinDP is established per TAB: open minimum OA damper, close economizer damper, measure OA flow, adjust linkage if needed to achieve design minimum, record DP as DesMinDP.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Instructions for establishing MinDP are given in the TAB specification. For example: 1. Open the minimum outdoor air damper and return air damper fully; close the economizer outdoor air damper. 2. Measure outdoor airflow. 3. If outdoor airflow rate is above design minimum (DesVot for ASHRAE Standard 62.1 or DesMinOA for California Title 24), adjust damper linkage on minimum outdoor air damper so that intake is at design minimum with damper fully stroked. 4. If outdoor airflow rate is below design minimum, temporarily adjust return air damper position via the BAS until design outdoor airflow is achieved. This position shall be used for testing only and shall not limit the return air damper position during normal operation. 5. Note DP across the outdoor air damper. This value becomes the design minimum outdoor air DP setpoint DesMinDP in the BAS. Convey this setpoint to BAS installer and note on air balance report.

candidate_entries.evidence_quote: Instructions for establishing MinDP are given in the TAB specification. For example: 1. Open the minimum outdoor air damper and return air damper fully; close the economizer outdoor air damper. 2. Measure outdoor airflow. 3. If outdoor airflow rate is above design minimum (DesVot for ASHRAE Standard 62.1 or DesMinOA for California Title 24), adjust damper linkage on minimum outdoor air damper so that intake is at design minimum with damper fully stroked. 4. If outdoor airflow rate is below design minimum, temporarily adjust return air damper position via the BAS until design outdoor airflow is achieved. This value becomes the design minimum outdoor air DP setpoint DesMinDP in the BAS. Convey this setpoint to BAS installer and note on air balance report.

structured_payload_candidate.title: TAB – Minimum Head Pressure Control Valve Position (Constant Speed CW Pumps)
structured_payload_candidate.equipment_scope: Water-cooled chiller plant with constant speed CW pumps
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Establish MinCWVlvPos by configuring plant for lowest chiller stage, throttling head pressure control valve until flow through chiller condenser drops to 110% of minimum flow switch setpoint. Record valve position.
structured_payload_candidate.commissioning_check: Verify MinCWVlvPos is established per TAB: configure lowest stage, throttle valve until flow is 110% of minimum, record position.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: For constant speed pumps, instructions for establishing MinCWVlvPos should be provided in the Test and Balance Specification. For example: 1. Configure the plant for the lowest stage of chiller operation. Open the head pressure control valve of the test chiller and enable a CW pump and the corresponding number of cooling towers for that stage. 2. Throttle the head pressure control valve of the test chiller in 2% increments until the flow rate through the chiller’s condenser decreases to 110% of the minimum condenser water flow switch setpoint as determined by condenser differential pressure. 3. Note the valve position setpoint and report to controls contractor.

candidate_entries.evidence_quote: For constant speed pumps, instructions for establishing MinCWVlvPos should be provided in the Test and Balance Specification. For example: 1. Configure the plant for the lowest stage of chiller operation. Open the head pressure control valve of the test chiller and enable a CW pump and the corresponding number of cooling towers for that stage. 2. Throttle the head pressure control valve of the test chiller in 2% increments until the flow rate through the chiller’s condenser decreases to 110% of the minimum condenser water flow switch setpoint as determined by condenser differential pressure. 3. Note the valve position setpoint and report to controls contractor.

candidate_entries.judge_reason: TAB procedure for minimum head pressure control valve position matched.
candidate_entries.review_notes: model_review_accepted: TAB procedure for minimum head pressure control valve position matched.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Equipment Staging and Rotation – Lead/Lag
structured_payload_candidate.equipment_scope: Parallel staged equipment (pumps, cooling towers, etc.)
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Lead/lag equipment shall be alternated when more than one is off or more than one is on so that equipment with most Staging Runtime is last stage and least is lead. Equipment are not hot-swapped during operation.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Lead/lag equipment: Unless otherwise noted, identical parallel staged equipment (such as CHW pumps and cooling towers) shall be lead/lag alternated when more than one is off or more than one is on so that the equipment with the most operating hours as determined by Staging Runtime is made the last stage equipment and the one with the least number of hours is made the lead stage equipment. This strategy effectively makes it such that equipment are not “hot swapped”, e.g., a pump would not be started and another stopped during operation just for runtime equalization.

candidate_entries.evidence_quote: Lead/lag equipment: Unless otherwise noted, identical parallel staged equipment (such as CHW pumps and cooling towers) shall be lead/lag alternated when more than one is off or more than one is on so that the equipment with the most operating hours as determined by Staging Runtime is made the last stage equipment and the one with the least number of hours is made the lead stage equipment. This strategy effectively makes it such that equipment are not “hot swapped”, e.g., a pump would not be started and another stopped during operation just for runtime equalization.

This strategy effectively makes it such that equipment are not “hot swapped”, e.g., a pump would not be started and another stopped during operation just for runtime equalization.
candidate_entries.judge_reason: Lead/lag staging rotation logic fully supported by evidence.
candidate_entries.review_notes: model_review_accepted: Lead/lag staging rotation logic fully supported by evidence.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Economizer High Limit Selection (ASHRAE 90.1)

structured_payload_candidate.title: Chilled Water Supply Temperature Reset – Differential Pressure Controlled Loops
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Reset CHWST setpoint and pump DP setpoint based on a Trim & Respond loop. From 0-50% loop output, reset DP setpoint from CHW-DPmin to CHW-DPmax. From 50-100% loop output, reset CHWST setpoint from CHWSTmax to CHWSTmin.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Map CHW Plant Reset loop output to DP setpoint (0-50%) and CHWST setpoint (50-100%) as described.
configurable_values: Trim & Respond parameters (SP0, SPmin, SPmax, Td, T, I, SPtrim, SPres, SPres-max)
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: a. From 0% loop output to 50% loop output, reset DP setpoint from CHW-DPmin to CHWP-DPmax. b. From 50% loop output to 100% loop output, reset CHWST setpoint from CHWSTmax to CHWSTmin.

structured_payload_candidate.title: CHW Plant – Pump or Tower Fan Alarm
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Pump or tower fan alarm if status differs from command for 15 seconds: commanded on but status off is Level 2 (delay 15 sec); commanded off but status on is Level 4 (delay 60 sec).
structured_payload_candidate.fault_condition: Status differs from command for 15 seconds.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Pump or tower fan alarm is indicated by the status input being different from the output command for 15 seconds. a. Commanded on, status off: Level 2. Do not evaluate alarm until the equipment has been commanded on for 15 seconds. b. Commanded off, status on: Level 4. Do not evaluate the alarm until the equipment has been commanded off for 60 seconds.

structured_payload_candidate.title: CHW Plant – FC#6: Chilled Water Supply Temperature Too High
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Fault condition: CHWST AVG - ƐCHWT ≥ CHWSTSP. Possible causes: mechanical problem with chillers, primary flow higher than design evaporator flow.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: FC#6 Equation CHWST AVG - ƐCHWT ≥ CHWSTSP Applies to OS #2 – #5 Description Chilled water supply temperature is too high Possible Diagnosis Mechanical problem with chillers Primary flow is higher than the design evaporator flow of the operating chillers

structured_payload_candidate.title: CHW Plant – FC#8: Condenser Approach Too High
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Fault condition: Approach COND ≥ RefrigCondTemp CH-x, AVG - CWRT CH-x, AVG. Possible causes: condenser fouling, blocked tubes, low CW temperature or flow.
